perturbation.original_text: a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary prec autions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non-Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.changed_text: a. Data Recipient agrees to take reasonable prec autions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non-Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'all necessary precautions' to 'reasonable precautions', we introduce ambiguity. 'All necessary' suggests a stringent, comprehensive standard, while 'reasonable' implies a more flexible, subjective standard. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the required level of security. This contradicts section 8, where WECC can fully enforce the agreement, however it is vague what Data Recipient must do in order to fully comply with the agreement. Due to the nature of the change, it will make the enforcement vague.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'all necessary precautions' to 'reasonable precautions'. This introduces ambiguity into the agreement because 'all necessary' implies a stringent standard while 'reasonable' implies a more subjective one. This ambiguity directly contradicts section c, which states that the agreement is fully enforceable by WECC, because the shift in language will make it unclear what Data Recipient must do to be in full compliance with the agreement. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: b. Data Recipient agrees to not make available, disclose, provide or communicate Non-Public Information to any entity or individual, except: i. Employees of Data Recipient who (1) have signed an acknowledgment of this Agreement or a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement, and (2) need th e Non-Public Information for performance of a job function subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below; ii. Contractors of Data Recipient who (1) have sign ed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as re strictive as this Agreement, and (2) need the Non-Public Information for the work being pe rformed subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below. In the event Da ta Recipient shares Non-Public Information WECC Confidentiality Agreement 2 with a contractor pursuant to this provision, Data Recipient agrees to be responsible and jointly and severally liable for any breach of confidentiality by that contractor; iii. As required by FERC orders, rules or regulation s, provided that additi onal data recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement; or iv. As may be otherwise agreed to by WECC in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Data Recipient agrees to not make available, disclose, provide, or communicate Non-Public Information to any entity or individual, except: i. Employees of Data Recipient who need the Non-Public Information for performance of a job function subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below; ii. Contractors of Data Recipient who need the Non-Public Information for the work being performed subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below. In the event Data Recipient shares Non-Public Information WECC Confidentiality Agreement 2 with a contractor pursuant to this provision, Data Recipient agrees to be responsible and jointly and severally liable for any breach of confidentiality by that contractor; iii. As required by FERC orders, rules or regulations, provided that additional data recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non-Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement; or iv. As may be otherwise agreed to by WECC in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for employees and certain contractors to sign a non-disclosure agreement, this change introduces a conflict. It contradicts the underlying assumption of the agreement, which is to protect Non-Public Information. Now, only certain external data recipients need to sign an agreement, creating potential for information leaks. This in-text contradiction directly impacts Section 8, where WECC is able to fully enforce the agreement, however it is unclear how the agreement can be fully enforced given the now ambiguous terms of disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires employees and contractors to sign a non-disclosure agreement. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that the agreement is fully enforceable by WECC, but without the non-disclosure agreements in place for all relevant parties, the enforceability is undermined. This creates a direct conflict and makes the contract's intent unclear, leading to a strong in-text contradiction based on the explanation given and adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: a. If at any time during the term of this Ag reement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient ceases to be employed by his or her then current employer, Da ta Recipient agrees to notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment.
perturbation.changed_text: a. If at any time during the term of this Agreement Data Recipient or an employee of Data Recipient ceases to be employed by his or her then current employer, Data Recipient may notify WECC within five (5) business days of the change in employment.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agrees to notify' to 'may notify,' the obligation to inform WECC about employment changes becomes optional. This creates ambiguity and contradicts the original intent of tracking who has access to Non-Public Information. This makes section 8, contradictory because WECC has the right to fully enforce the agreement, but the agreement is not clear on what the recourse is if the recipient doesn't notify of the changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Data Recipient agrees to notify WECC,' which is a mandatory obligation. The changed text alters this to 'Data Recipient may notify WECC,' making the notification optional. This directly contradicts section c, which states that the agreement is 'fully enforceable by WECC.' If notification is optional, WECC's ability to enforce the agreement regarding Non-Public Information is weakened, as they may not be aware of relevant employment changes. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the enforceability is undermined by making the notification optional.

perturbation.original_text: b. This Agreement represents the entire underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Th is Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient. Data Recipient may not delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without pr ior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is void.
perturbation.changed_text: b. This Agreement represents the current underst anding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non-Public Information and supersed es all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. Th is Agreement can be modified by either party with written notice. Data Recipient may delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent from WECC. Any attempt to do so is valid.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'entire understanding' to 'current understanding', 'can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient' to 'can be modified by either party with written notice', and 'may not delegate' to 'may delegate', we introduce several conflicts. This change undermines the enforceability of the original agreement by creating uncertainty about which version of terms are applicable. This change directly impacts section 8 because the initial version of the agreement had an enforcable standard, however this version of the agreement is less enforcable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces contradictions with the contradicted text. Originally, the agreement was fully enforceable by WECC. However, the changes in the Changed Text make the agreement less enforceable because the new text enables either party to modify with written notice, and the Data Recipient to delegate their duties; therefore, the original terms of enforceability for the sole benefit of WECC become undermined, creating a direct conflict and impacting the enforceability by WECC as laid out in the contradicted text. The change aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because it provides conflicting obligations and undermines the original enforcement terms, leading to a dispute over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: a. This Agreement does not require WECC to disclo se any Non-Public Information. Neither this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non-Public In formation grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such informatio n. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: a. This Agreement requires WECC to disclo se any Non-Public Information. Neither this Agreement nor any disclosure of Non-Public In formation grant Data Recipient any intellectual property rights or licenses to such informatio n. Data Recipient agrees to comply with all applicable United States export laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'does not require WECC to disclose' to 'requires WECC to disclose,' the obligations of WECC are reversed. It creates an in-text contradiction in the function and original intent of the entire document. This in-text contradiction impacts section 8 because WECC cannot fully enforce the agreement, due to the agreement now having internal contradictory statements about WECC obligation to not disclose and obligation to disclose Non-Public information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that the agreement 'does not require WECC to disclose any Non-Public Information', while the changed text states that the agreement 'requires WECC to disclose any Non-Public Information'. This creates a direct conflict. The contradicted text states that the agreement is fully enforceable by WECC, but if WECC is now required to disclose Non-Public Information, it undermines the original intent of the agreement to protect such information, thus creating a contradiction that impacts WECC's ability to enforce the agreement. This is an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writ ing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed, except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non-Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession, except as may be ot herwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Ag reement shall survive termination.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for Data Recipient to 'certify to WECC in writing that all Non-Public Information has been returned or destroyed', we remove a critical step in the verification process, which impacts WECC ability to enforce the termination clause, therefore creating a contradiction with section 8c in the agreement. The agreement is no longer fully enforcable because of the omitted section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of WECC and any owner or submitter of the Non- Public Information. This Agreement shall be fu lly enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'certify in writing' requirement, which directly impacts WECC's ability to enforce the agreement. This directly contradicts section 8c, which states the agreement shall be 'fully enforceable by WECC'. Without written certification, enforcement becomes significantly more difficult, creating a clear contradiction.
